Your Majesty,�Greetings of the day.
My name is Yashvardhan Yadav, a respected resident of the United Arab Emirates since 27/05/2024. With due respect, I wish to bring to your kind attention a matter that has caused me great distress and hardship.
I applied for a driving license in August 2025, and now in 2026, I find myself still unsuccessful due to repeated failures by the driving school. I strongly feel that the institute has been unfairly failing students without valid or genuine reasons. To date, I have paid approximately AED 5,800 in fees. Most recently, I was failed in the final road test despite not making any significant mistake, and the examiner’s behavior was also discourteous and unprofessional.
Your Majesty, until this experience, my journey in the UAE under your gracious leadership has been truly wonderful. However, this incident has left me feeling a sense of injustice. I have exhausted all my savings paying the driving school’s fees and am now in a situation where I do not even have sufficient funds to book a flight to return home.
Obtaining a driving license was my only hope to secure a stable livelihood and continue surviving here. Unfortunately, I have now lost that opportunity as well.
I humbly request your kind consideration and intervention in this matter, so that justice may be served.
With deepest respect and regards,
Yashvardhan Yadav

Letter to the Royal Family of the United Arab Emirates
 
 
Your Highness Sheikh Mohamed bin Zayed Al Nahyan,
 
My name is Ivan, I am from Almaty, Kazakhstan. I am a professional beekeeper. The honey produced by my bees is unique due to its origin. The bees collect nectar in high mountain regions rich in medicinal herbs, which makes my honey not only delicious but also highly beneficial for health.
 
The purpose of my visit to the UAE is to present Your Highnesses with this unique, healing honey. If you appreciate its quality, I am ready to deliver annually between 500 to 1500 kg of this honey. Due to the specific highland location of the apiary, larger volumes are not possible, which guarantees the exclusivity of the product.
 
In addition to honey, I can also offer the following bee products:
 
- Perga (Bee Bread) — a natural product rich in amino acids, vitamins, minerals, and enzymes. It supports the immune system, improves blood circulation, and strengthens overall health.
- Zabrus — a valuable beekeeping product containing beeswax, honey, pollen, and propolis. It is traditionally used to support oral health, strengthen immunity, and act as a natural antimicrobial remedy.
- Drone Homogenate (Apilarnil) — a biologically active natural product rich in proteins, hormones, and vitamins. It is used to improve vitality, increase energy, and support male health.
 
At present, I am staying at Al Khoory Atrium Hotel, Dubai. Should my products be of interest, I would be honored to personally meet and present them. Furthermore, I am ready to return to the UAE at Your Highnesses’ invitation at any convenient time.
 
Due to my limited English, I kindly ask that any communication be made via WhatsApp or email.
 
My contacts:
WhatsApp: +7 707 116 4400
Email: bsbuild2015@gmail.com
 
With the deepest respect, Ivan

A few days ago, I lost my father. I was supposed to travel to bury him, but I could not because of some civil cases. I chose to pay all the amounts required, without waiting for judgments, just so I could go and bury my father—because this is something I can never forgive myself if I miss it. It was not easy, but I managed to pay.

To complete the process, I had to postpone my father’s burial from last week to this Monday. When I was finally ready to travel on Monday, I discovered I had another travel ban from last year, related to a case with my ex-girlfriend, who is outside the country. That case stayed at the police station for a year without being sent to the prosecutor. They had asked her at the time to go for a medical report, but she never followed up. Honestly, I never touched her—the only thing I did was break her phone after I caught her cheating.

Because of this, I had to postpone my father’s burial a second time to Tuesday. I tried to resolve it by contacting my ex, asking her to give power of attorney to someone inside the country so they could withdraw the case. That was the only way I could fly tonight. But instead of helping, even knowing my father passed away, she took advantage of me and demanded a large sum of money.

Now I cannot postpone the burial again. My uncles, brothers, and sisters cannot accept any more delays, and my father has already been in the mortuary for days. I feel helpless. That is why I am writing this comment here under His Highness’s post—hoping to get help so I can still catch my flight tonight.

If God really wants me to bury my father, His Highness will see this comment and allow me to travel for a few days and return. This is the only help I can seek now. I write this message helpless, but still believing that God works in mysterious ways, and His miracles have no limit. Whoever sees this message, please tag His Highness—so that maybe by God’s strength I can be able to bury my father tomorrow.

قبل أيام قليلة فقدت والدي، وكنت مستعداً للسفر لكنني لم أستطع بسبب بعض القضايا المدنية. اضطررت أن أنهيها وأدفع المبالغ المستحقة دون انتظار الأحكام حتى أتمكن من السفر ودفن والدي، لأنني أشعر أنني لن أسامح نفسي أبداً إذا لم أفعل ذلك. دفعت كل المبالغ رغم أن الأمر لم يكن سهلاً. ولإتمام الإجراءات اضطررت أن أؤجل دفن والدي من الأسبوع الماضي إلى هذا الأسبوع يوم الاثنين.

وعندما كنت مستعداً للسفر يوم الاثنين، اكتشفت أن هناك منع سفر آخر من العام الماضي، بسبب قضية مع صديقتي السابقة، وهي خارج الدولة. هذه القضية بقيت سنة كاملة في مركز الشرطة ولم تُحال إلى النيابة. وقد طُلب من هذه السيدة أن تذهب إلى الإدارة الطبية لفحص ما إذا كانت لديها إصابات، ولكنها لم تفعل. وأنا صادق لم ألمسها أبداً، الشيء الوحيد الذي أتذكره أنني كسرت هاتفها عندما اكتشفت خيانتها.

وهذا أجبرني أن أؤجل دفن والدي مرة ثانية ليوم الثلاثاء. حاولت التواصل مع صديقتي السابقة بدلاً من انتظار إحالة القضية، لأن النيابة يمكنها إغلاق الملف فقط بعد إحالته للمحكمة وهذا يحتاج أياماً. فاخترت الطريق الأسهل وهو أن توقع توكيلاً لشخص موجود في الدولة حتى يسحب الشكوى. وكان هذا الخيار الوحيد الذي يمكن أن يسمح لي بالطيران الليلة.

لكن بعد أن عرفت أن والدي قد توفي، استغلت ظروفي وطلبت مني مبلغاً كبيراً من المال مقابل أن توقع على التوكيل. وعندما أدركت أنني لا أستطيع، فات الأوان، واضطررت أن أؤجل دفن والدي للمرة الثالثة. لكنني لم أتمكن من ذلك بسبب عمي وإخوتي وأخواتي، لأن والدي ظل في ثلاجة الموتى أياماً طويلة.

أنا عاجز الآن، ولهذا أكتب هذا التعليق تحت منشور سموكم على الإنستغرام لعلي أجد مساعدة وأتمكن من اللحاق برحلتي الليلة. إذا كان الله يريد لي أن أدفن والدي، فسوف يرى سموكم هذا التعليق ويسمح لي بالسفر لبضعة أيام ثم العودة. هذه هي المساعدة الوحيدة التي أستطيع أن أرجوها. أكتب هذه الكلمات وأنا في قمة العجز، ولكنني ما زلت مؤمناً أن الله يعمل بطرق عجيبة، ومعجزاته لا حدود لها. ومن يرى هذا التعليق يمكنه أن يضع إشارة لسموكم، لعلها تكون سبباً في أن أدفن والدي غداً.

they have fabricated a case against us and intend, under the pretext of installing an accordion door (which is a security gate), to send several armed men and police to attack our home and make us disappear / kill us.
This has always been the excuse.
Once, the Iraqi family with the surname Tahami — whose daughter is the wife of the head of Tehran’s Bazaar Police — came to our residence. At that time we were also tenants of an Iraqi woman named Fazileh Nakhchi.
We were living in District 12 of Tehran, Sar-Cheshmeh intersection, Bahre-ol-Olum Alley, No. 27, in 2020. Under the pretext of moving a large sofa, they knocked on our door and said the sofa was large and asked us to open the door so they could carry it up the stair landing to the upper floor. We did not open the door. The second daughter of the Tahami family spoke very abusively to us.
Now, after two years of living in this building, our landlord has forced us — in favor of the Iraqis and the employees of the United Nations in Iran, who all live in District 12 — under the pretext of installing an accordion security door, to have them come and attack our home.
We have no security from the United Nations employees in Iran who are Iraqi and Pashtun, nor from the police of District 12 of Tehran who all work at the United Nations in Iran.

Yes, of course. Here is the Persian translation of those two English texts, combined into one continuous and coherent passage:
Some Afghans who are registered with the United Nations — especially from Qarabagh, Ghazni — claim to be the uncles, aunts, and cousins (sons and daughters of uncles and aunts) of my sister Soheila and me. They are lying. Because they have influence inside the United Nations, and either they themselves or their relatives are employed in the United States and the Pentagon, they think that with the help of the residents of District 12 of Tehran and the Iraqis they can deceive prominent people.
Their so-called “evidence” of kinship is that they say they have spoken with my father, or that my sister Soheila, my mother, and I attended several gatherings of these Afghans as invited guests. From photos and videos of our presence there, they fabricated a case claiming they are our uncles and aunts — especially Davood Gholam Nabi and his wife Fatemeh Amini, along with their two daughters and two sons named Aida and Mina Amini (residing in Sweden), Farshad (residing in the United States), and Farhad Amini. They also include the daughters of his brothers and sisters who live in Sweden, particularly Khatimeh Akbari who lives in Stockholm, Sweden.
The Afghans with the family name Akbari, the Iraqis with the family name Akbari, and the Kurds with the family name Amini claim to own the Instagram accounts of my sister Soheila and me, as well as our identities.
Please remove and expel all Afghans, Iraqis, and Iranians from the United Nations, and strip them of their qualifications, influence, and power.
They forced my father to confirm and introduce these Afghans, as well as the Rashti, Kurdish, Iraqi, and Pashtun relatives, as his own brothers and sisters.

Story: The Rope of Unity
Once, a traveler saw three men trying to pull a heavy stone. Each pulled from a different side, and the stone did not move. Then, they tied their ropes together and pulled in the same direction. To their surprise, the stone moved with ease.

The traveler said: “Alone you are weak, but together you are unbreakable.”

Why Middle Eastern Unity Matters
	1.	Shared Resources – Water, energy, and trade routes are blessings for the whole region. Together, countries can protect and develop them fairly.
	2.	Economic Power – When the Middle East works as one, it becomes stronger in global markets, reducing dependence on outsiders.
	3.	Cultural and Religious Bonds – The region shares faith, history, and traditions that naturally connect its people.
	4.	Peace and Stability – Division invites outside interference, but unity builds security from within.
	5.	Global Respect – A united Middle East has a stronger voice in international organizations and can shape its own future.

The lesson: Breaking unity weakens the whole region. Preserving partnerships is not only wisdom — it is also a responsibility, so that future generations inherit strength, not division.

Who is right, the Jews or the Palestinians? Answer from GPT: … both peoples are right.
Some facts:
The Jews consider the land of Israel their historical homeland (since biblical times). After the Holocaust and centuries of persecution, they established the State of Israel in 1948.
The Palestinians are a people who have lived in these territories for hundreds of years. They also consider this land their homeland and strive to have their own state.
The essence of the conflict:
The Jews say: “We returned to our land so that we would never again be expelled or destroyed.”
The Palestinians say: “Our homes and our lands were taken from us, and we became refugees.”
Who is right?
Both sides have truth on their side.
The Jews have the right to security and their own state.
The Palestinians have the right to freedom and their own state.
The biggest problem is that politics, wars, and hatred prevent finding a compromise. And it is always ordinary people who suffer: children, women, civilians.
👉 Therefore, the answer is: both peoples have the right to life, security, and dignity. And the real truth will come when instead of asking “who is right?” the world asks: “how can we make it so that both peoples live in peace, happiness, and without fear?”
The world must organize a good country for the Palestinians so that they can live happily.

A Blood Moon eclipse is considered a powerful time to make wishes.

A Blood Moon eclipse is also interpreted as the approach of the apocalypse.
In the Quran, in verse 37 of Surah Fussilat, the sun and moon, night and day, are among the proofs of God's unity and power.

@eleonorashah777 For the whole world it is just a beautiful picture. Well, the Moon. Well, red. Impressive.
But perhaps, just like yesterday’s story, this video carries a meaning.
The Moon is the one who is far away and has stopped coming into contact. But the red color here is important: it symbolizes not only passion and love, but also anger and irritation. In tradition, red is always the color of heightened emotions. Even in emojis 😡 we see a red face — a sign of anger.
That is why I read it this way for myself: the Moon is more likely a woman than a man. She is far away, and her feelings are no longer cold but flaring up, filled with resentment or anger.
😍We never cease to be amazed by the space outside of Earth. It is beautiful to witness these phenomena.

Beneath the fading crimson sky,
a kingdom breathes in silence deep.

whispers of dreams the desert keeps.

a mirror cast on time’s still sea.
Through eclipse, the shadow bends,
yet hope remains in majesty.

loyal eyes toward your guiding flame.

forever speaking your noble name.✨💛💫
